response: The three main psychedelic compounds found in Psilocybin genus mushrooms that are converted to psilocin by the body are psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin. Psilocybin is converted to psilocin through metabolic processing, while baeocystin is a phosphorylated derivative of 4-HO-NMT and is converted to psilocin as well. Psilocin remains unchanged in this conversion process.
